touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Carter Schneider at CobaltMedia. No answer, left voicemail introducing AskElephant and mentioning pipeline visibility challenges for fintech sales teams.
next_steps: Follow up with second call attempt

touches.ai_summary: Second cold call attempt to Carter Schneider. No answer, left brief voicemail referencing previous message and offering to share relevant case study.
Brief connection with Carter Schneider. Introduced AskElephant's pipeline visibility solution. Carter expressed initial interest but had limited time. Agreed to follow-up email with more details.

questions_asked.question: Are you currently facing challenges with pipeline visibility across your sales team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have some gaps in our current reporting

next_steps: Send follow-up email with case study and meeting invite

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with fintech case study and meeting scheduling link. Carter responded positively, noting their team of ~20 sales reps struggles with forecast accuracy. Scheduled qualification call.

questions_asked.question: What size teams do you typically work with?
questions_asked.answer: We work with teams from 10-200+ seats, with strong results in the 15-50 range

next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Carter Schneider. Discussed CobaltMedia's current state: using basic CRM reporting, 20-person sales team, challenges with deal stage accuracy and rep accountability. Carter is Head of Sales, reports to CEO. Budget appears available for Q2.

questions_asked.question: What's your current tech stack for sales analytics?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly built-in CRM dashboards, some manual spreadsheets

questions_asked.question: Who else would be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: I'd need buy-in from our CEO Layla, and our implementation team would need to be involved

touches.ai_summary: Second qualification call diving into use cases. Carter shared specific pain points: deals stalling in demo stage, inconsistent data entry by reps, difficulty identifying at-risk deals early. Discussed timeline (ideally implemented by end of Q2).

questions_asked.question: What happens when deals stall currently - how do you identify and address them?
questions_asked.answer: Usually by the time we notice, it's too late. We're reactive rather than proactive

questions_asked.question: Does your solution require significant CRM changes or new data entry from reps?
questions_asked.answer: No, we pull from existing CRM data and use AI to surface insights automatically

next_steps: Schedule full discovery with use case deep-dive

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Carter Schneider. Mapped out detailed requirements: 19 seats (sales leadership + reps), focus on automations for pipeline visibility and at-risk deal identification. Carter mentioned they're not evaluating other vendors currently. Discussed implementation timeline and need for some consulting support on process optimization.

questions_asked.question: Beyond the core team, are there other stakeholders like RevOps or implementation leads who should be involved?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have implementation leads Arjun and Oliver who would handle rollout, plus Harper our Sales Manager

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for you in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Better forecast accuracy, earlier visibility into at-risk deals, and less time in spreadsheets

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: 4-6 weeks for full rollout with consulting package

next_steps: Send demo invite including technical stakeholders
Prepare custom demo focused on fintech use case

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email summarizing discovery findings and confirming demo attendees. Shared demo agenda focused on pipeline visibility automations and at-risk deal detection. Carter confirmed Arjun, Harper, and Oliver will attend technical evaluation.
next_steps: Prepare custom fintech dataset for demo

touches.ai_summary: Email check-in before technical evaluation. Carter confirmed team is excited about demo. Mentioned they're moving quickly to hit Q2 implementation goal.
next_steps: Technical evaluation demo confirmed for tomorrow

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo email sent with login credentials and agenda. Carter acknowledged and confirmed all stakeholders will attend.

Technical evaluation with Carter, Arjun, Harper, and Oliver. Walked through automations_plus_consulting package features. Team engaged positively. Arjun asked detailed questions about data integration. Harper liked the at-risk deal scoring. Some discussion about whether consulting hours would be sufficient for their process optimization needs.

questions_asked.question: How does the data sync work with our CRM - is it real-time?
questions_asked.answer: Near real-time, typically 15-minute refresh cycle

questions_asked.question: What's included in the consulting package - is it enough to help us redesign our stage definitions?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, includes process audit and stage optimization as part of onboarding

objections: Team needs to confirm the consulting hours are adequate for the level of process work they want to do
next_steps: Loop in CEO Layla for exec alignment

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting with CEO Layla King and Carter Schneider. Layla asked strategic questions about ROI, competitive differentiation for CobaltMedia, and implementation risk. Discussed business case: improving forecast accuracy by 20% and reducing deal slippage. Layla expressed support but wants to see final pricing before approval. Conversation was thorough and detail-oriented.

questions_asked.question: What ROI are other fintech companies seeing in the first year?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 3-5x through improved close rates and reduced deal cycle time

questions_asked.question: What's the risk if implementation doesn't go smoothly?
questions_asked.answer: We have a phased rollout approach and dedicated success manager to minimize disruption

questions_asked.question: Are there any other evaluation criteria or stakeholders we should address?
questions_asked.answer: No, if the pricing works for our Q2 budget, we're ready to move forward

next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing breakdown
Schedule proposal review call with Carter

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Carter Schneider (rescheduled from earlier in week). Presented pricing: $16,400 ACV for 19 seats with automations_plus_consulting. Carter expressed concern about budget - mentioned they had allocated $14K. Brief discussion about package options and value justification.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the per-seat pricing given our commitment to the consulting package?
questions_asked.answer: Let me discuss with leadership and get back to you

objections: Pricing is above their initial budget allocation

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Carter Schneider. Discussed pricing flexibility and value drivers. Carter reiterated $14K target and asked about reducing scope. Walked through options: fewer seats, reducing consulting hours, or annual vs quarterly payment. Carter interested in keeping full package but needs discount to get to budget. Agreed to propose adjusted pricing.

questions_asked.question: If we commit to annual upfront payment, is there additional discount available?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can offer 15-20% for annual prepay depending on final terms

questions_asked.question: Is the $14K budget flexible at all or is it a hard ceiling?
questions_asked.answer: There's a little room but not much - maybe $14.5K max

objections: Budget constraint limiting ability to move forward at list price
next_steps: Get leadership approval for adjusted pricing

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with revised proposal: 21% discount bringing ACV to $16,400 (appears to be error in calculation, likely meant to reflect negotiated rate closer to budget). Carter acknowledged receipt and said he needs to review with Layla before confirming.
next_steps: Carter to review with CEO Layla

touches.ai_summary: Brief executive check-in with CEO Layla King and Carter Schneider. Layla reviewed revised pricing and terms. Confirmed the numbers work within their Q2 budget with the applied discount. Layla asked about contract terms and payment schedule. Overall positive signal to move forward pending final pricing call.

questions_asked.question: What are the payment terms - can we do quarterly instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, quarterly payment available though annual prepay offers better discount

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call with Carter Schneider. Locked in terms: $16,400 ACV, 19 seats, automations_plus_consulting package, 21% discount applied, quarterly payment schedule. Carter confirmed verbal approval from Layla. Discussed contract review process and anticipated signature timeline. Target start date early May to hit Q2 implementation goal.

questions_asked.question: What's your contract review process and timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Should be 3-5 business days for legal review, then we can sign

questions_asked.question: When can we realistically kick off implementation?
questions_asked.answer: First week of May, giving us 8 weeks before end of Q2
